steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The subject of the sentence is 'National Heritage Areas' — a plural noun. The intervening phrase 'like the Arabia Mountain National Heritage Area in Georgia' modifies the subject but does not change its number. The blank needs a verb that agrees with a plural subject.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'has been': Singular. 'Has been' pairs with a singular subject (e.g., 'it has been'), not a plural one. ✗
  • Choice B — 'are': Plural present tense. 'National Heritage Areas… are officially designated' is grammatically correct subject–verb agreement. ✓
  • Choice C — 'was': Singular past tense. Fails on both number and tense — the passage describes an ongoing official designation, not a past singular event. ✗
  • Choice D — 'is': Singular present tense. Does not agree with the plural subject 'National Heritage Areas.' ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
